176	model Founder {
177	  id             String       @id @default(cuid())
178	  organizationId String
179	  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
180	
181	  name                    String
182	  username                String
183	  email                   String
184	  passwordHash            String
185	  // Role ladder — see src/lib/roles.ts for the predicate helpers and
186	  // the full description of each tier:
187	  //   admin   → full read + write + can change other founders' roles
188	  //   founder → full read + write
189	  //   viewer  → read-only; every write API returns 403 and every UI
190	  //              affordance for writes is hidden
191	  // Default "founder" preserves prior behaviour: anyone added via
192	  // scripts/add-founder.ts is read+write unless explicitly seeded
193	  // with ADD_FOUNDER_ROLE=viewer or =admin. Validation lives at the
194	  // application layer; the column stays TEXT so adding a future tier
195	  // is a docstring + helper change, not a migration.
196	  role                    String    @default("founder") // admin | founder | viewer
197	  bio                     String?
198	  displayName             String?
199	  roleTitle               String?
200	  publicUrl               String?
201	  avatar                  String?
202	  accountNudgeDismissedAt DateTime?
203	
204	  /// Maps to FounderProfile.id in Noosphere
205	  noosphereId String?
206	
207	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
208	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
209	
210	  sessions                   Session[]
211	  uploads                    Upload[]
212	  auditEvents                AuditEvent[]
213	  deletionRequests           DeletionRequest[]
214	  attributedConclusions      Conclusion[]                @relation("ConclusionFounder")
215	  researchSuggestions        ResearchSuggestion[]
216	  reviewResolutions          ReviewItem[]                @relation("ReviewResolver")
217	  publicationReviews         PublicationReview[]         @relation("PublicationReviewer")
218	  recalibrationOverrides     RecalibrationOverride[]
219	  apiKeys                    ApiKey[]
220	  conclusionDeletionRequests ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
221	  dashboardDismissals        DashboardDismissal[]
222	  resolvedContradictions     Contradiction[]             @relation("ContradictionResolver")
223	  revisionEvents             RevisionEvent[]
224	  publicReplies              PublicReply[]               @relation("PublicReplyFounder")
225	
226	  @@unique([organizationId, email])
227	  @@unique([organizationId, username])
228	  @@index([organizationId])
229	}
230	
231	/// Browser session (server-side row; cookie carries signed opaque token)
232	model Session {
233	  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
234	  organizationId String
235	  founderId      String
236	  token          String   @unique
237	  expiresAt      DateTime
238	  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
239	
240	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
241	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
242	
243	  @@index([organizationId])
244	  @@index([founderId])
245	}
246	
247	// ── Uploads ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248	
249	model Upload {
250	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
251	  organizationId String
252	  founderId      String
253	
254	  title            String
255	  description      String?
256	  sourceType       String   @default("written") // written | annotation | external | transcript
257	  originalName     String
258	  mimeType         String
259	  filePath         String
260	  fileSize         Int
261	  textContent      String?
262	  blurb            String?
263	  // Status ladder (see src/lib/uploadStatus.ts for the canonical
264	  // vocabulary + UI labels). `pending | processing | ingested | failed`
265	  // are load-bearing strings from the init migration; `extracting` and
266	  // `awaiting_ingest` were added alongside the Wave-1 audio/PDF
267	  // extractors so founders can see which stage of the pipeline a row is
268	  // in (Whisper/pypdf vs. claim extraction) instead of a single opaque
269	  // "processing".
270	  status           String   @default("pending") // pending | extracting | awaiting_ingest | processing | ingested | failed
271	  processLog       String   @default("") // streamed CLI stdout/stderr
272	  claimsCount      Int?
273	  methodCount      Int?
274	  substCount       Int?
275	  principleCount   Int?
276	  errorMessage     String?
277	  // How the text was obtained for this upload, set by the noosphere
278	  // extractor dispatcher on success. Free-form string — e.g.
279	  // "passthrough", "faster-whisper", "openai-whisper-1", "pypdf",
280	  // "ocrmypdf". Rendered in the dashboard detail row so the founder
281	  // knows whether a transcript/PDF extract was reliable.
282	  extractionMethod String?
283	  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())
284	  updatedAt        DateTime @updatedAt
285	
286	  // ── Public blog fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────
287	  // An Upload becomes a public blog post when `publishedAt IS NOT NULL`.
288	  // Default is null → the upload remains a private founder artifact.
289	  // `slug` is the URL-safe identifier used for `/post/:slug`; it's
290	  // generated from `title` at publish time and guaranteed unique across
291	  // the whole table (not per-org — the blog is a single public surface).
292	  // `blogExcerpt` is the card/list summary shown on the index; if null
293	  // we derive one from the first ~280 chars of `textContent`.
294	  // `authorBio` lets the founder override the displayed byline (e.g.
295	  // for guest posts or co-authored work); otherwise we show
296	  // Founder.name.
297	  publishedAt DateTime?
298	  slug        String?   @unique
299	  blogExcerpt String?
300	  authorBio   String?
301	
302	  // ── Soft delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
303	  // `deletedAt` is the single source of truth for "this upload has
304	  // been removed". Every query that surfaces uploads to the UI must
305	  // filter by `deletedAt IS NULL`; we keep the row around so the audit
306	  // trail (AuditEvent pointing at this upload) stays intact, and so a
307	  // DB-level restore is possible if a deletion turns out to be a
308	  // mistake. Only the uploader can set this directly; peers must go
309	  // through a DeletionRequest, which the owner accepts (setting
310	  // `deletedAt=now`) or declines.
311	  deletedAt DateTime?
312	
313	  // ── Visibility inside the firm ─────────────────────────────────────
314	  // Three-level visibility ladder. Every value still lets Noosphere
315	  // run ingestion — conclusions, contradictions, open questions, and
316	  // research suggestions are always extracted. The levels only
317	  // control *who reads the upload's metadata and contents*.
318	  //
319	  // "org"          → visible to every founder in the organization AND
320	  //                   eligible for blog publication. The default;
321	  //                   library, dashboard, peer deletion requests, and
322	  //                   the blog's publishAsPost toggle all apply.
323	  //
324	  // "semi-private" → visible to every founder in the organization,
325	  //                   but never publishable to the public blog. The
326	  //                   intermediate level, for material the firm wants
327	  //                   to discuss internally but that must never leak
328	  //                   outside. /api/publish rejects the flip; the
329	  //                   public blog index + /post/[slug] both filter
330	  //                   it out; /library and /dashboard render it with
331	  //                   a "semi-private" badge so peers know the
332	  //                   constraint.
333	  //
334	  // "private"      → only the uploader can see the row anywhere in
335	  //                   the UI; Noosphere still ingests it so the owner
336	  //                   gets their own conclusions/contradictions, but
337	  //                   nobody else can see the file, read it, or open
338	  //                   a deletion request against it. Blog-publishing
339	  //                   a private upload is rejected at the API layer
340	  //                   — flip visibility first.
341	  //
342	  // Column stays TEXT with default "org" so no migration is needed
343	  // when new levels are introduced; validation happens at the API
344	  // layer.
345	  visibility String @default("org")
346	
347	  // ── Podcast / audio playback ───────────────────────────────────────
348	  // When an audio upload is stored in a public CDN-addressable
349	  // location (Supabase Storage in our case), we persist the public
350	  // URL here so /post/[slug] can render a <audio controls> player
351	  // alongside the transcript. `audioDurationSec` is optional
352	  // metadata derived from ffprobe / client-side duration detection
353	  // when available; the UI falls back to the player's own duration
354	  // readout if it's null. Both fields are null for non-audio rows
355	  // and for audio rows uploaded before Supabase Storage was
356	  // configured — the blog post still renders, just text-only.
357	  audioUrl         String?
358	  audioDurationSec Int?
359	
360	  founder             Founder              @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id])
361	  organization        Organization         @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
362	  chunks              UploadChunk[]
363	  auditEvents         AuditEvent[]
364	  deletionRequests    DeletionRequest[]
365	  // ── Provenance back-refs ─────────────────────────────────────────
366	  // Derived entities (Conclusions via the M:N join, Contradictions,
367	  // OpenQuestions, ResearchSuggestions directly) point back here so
368	  // the delete path can cascade-clean any artifact whose source has
369	  // been removed. `conclusionSources` is the many-to-many bridge —
370	  // the same text can be extracted from multiple uploads, and each
371	  // upload holds its own row in the bridge. The direct sourceUpload
372	  // relations on the other three entities are one-per-row: a
373	  // contradiction / open-question / research suggestion is an output
374	  // of exactly one ingestion run, even when it references claims from
375	  // multiple uploads.
376	  conclusionSources   ConclusionSource[]
377	  methodologyProfiles MethodologyProfile[]
378	  contradictions      Contradiction[]      @relation("ContradictionSourceUpload")
379	  openQuestions       OpenQuestion[]       @relation("OpenQuestionSourceUpload")
380	  researchSuggestions ResearchSuggestion[] @relation("ResearchSuggestionSourceUpload")
381	
382	  @@index([organizationId])
383	  @@index([founderId])
384	  @@index([publishedAt])
385	  @@index([deletedAt])
386	  @@index([visibility])
387	  // Dashboard filters by status constantly (ingested rows on the main
388	  // panel, failed/pending counts on the banner). A plain btree index
389	  // is enough — the org-scope is already covered by organizationId.
390	  @@index([status])
391	}
392	
393	model UploadChunk {
394	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
395	  uploadId     String
396	  index        Int
397	  text         String
398	  startMs      Int?
399	  endMs        Int?
400	  speakerLabel String?
401	  headingHint  String?
402	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
403	  updatedAt    DateTime @updatedAt
404	
405	  upload Upload @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
406	
407	  @@unique([uploadId, index])
408	  @@index([uploadId])
409	}
410	
411	// ── DeletionRequest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
412	// Peer-to-peer "please delete this" workflow. A founder who doesn't own
413	// an upload but wants it removed opens a request against that upload;
414	// the owner reviews it (accept → soft-delete the upload, decline → log
415	// reason and keep). The requester can cancel their own request at any
416	// time before the owner responds.
417	//
418	// Enforcement:
419	//   * Requester.organizationId must equal Upload.organizationId (done
420	//     at route-handler level, not schema — cross-org requests are
421	//     structurally unreachable via UI anyway).
422	//   * Requester.id MUST NOT equal Upload.founderId (an owner deletes
423	//     their own upload directly via DELETE /api/uploads/:id; opening
424	//     a request against yourself is noise).
425	//   * At most one ACTIVE (status=pending) request per (upload, requester)
426	//     pair — the unique partial-index is added in the migration SQL.
427	
428	model DeletionRequest {
429	  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
430	  uploadId    String
431	  requesterId String
432	  status      String    @default("pending") // pending | accepted | declined | cancelled
433	  reason      String?
434	  decision    String? // optional note from the owner when accepting/declining
435	  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
436	  respondedAt DateTime?
437	
438	  upload    Upload  @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
439	  requester Founder @relation(fields: [requesterId], references: [id])
440	
441	  @@index([uploadId])
442	  @@index([requesterId])
443	  @@index([status])
444	}
445	
446	// ── Conclusion — mirrors noosphere.models.Conclusion ───────────────────────
447	
448	model Conclusion {
449	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
450	  organizationId String
451	
452	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
453	  noosphereId String? @unique
454	  text        String
455

1168	model PublicResponse {
1169	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
1170	  organizationId String
1171	
1172	  publishedConclusionId String
1173	
1174	  /// counter_evidence | counter_argument | clarification | agreement_extension
1175	  kind           String
1176	  body           String
1177	  citationUrl    String  @default("")
1178	  submitterEmail String  @default("")
1179	  orcid          String  @default("")
1180	  pseudonymous   Boolean @default(false)
1181	
1182	  /// Did the responder consent to having their response published if
1183	  /// the firm decides to reply publicly? Captured per-response on the
1184	  /// submission form (Round 17 prompt 31). When false, the founder
1185	  /// queue suppresses the "Promote to PublicReply" affordance and the
1186	  /// public-article appendix never renders this row.
1187	  publishConsent Boolean @default(false)
1188	
1189	  /// pending | approved | rejected | engaged | archived
1190	  status        String @default("pending")
1191	  moderatorNote String @default("")
1192	
1193	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
1194	  seenAt    DateTime?
1195	
1196	  organization Organization        @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
1197	  published    PublishedConclusion @relation(fields: [publishedConclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
1198	  triage       ResponseTriage?
1199	  publicReply  PublicReply?
1200	
1201	  @@index([organizationId])
1202	  @@index([publishedConclusionId])
1203	  @@index([status])
1204	  @@index([seenAt])
1205	}
1206	
1207	// ── Round 17 prompt 31: response triage + dialog loop ──────────────────────
1208	//
1209	// `ResponseTriage` is a 1:1 sidecar to `PublicResponse` written by the
1210	// Python classifier in `noosphere/literature/response_triage.py`. The
1211	// triage row is the queue's source of priority (substantive vs noise);
1212	// the founder retains the override via `manualLabel` / `manualReason`.
1213	//
1214	// The plaintext submitter email is NEVER duplicated here; we only keep
1215	// the SHA-256 hash so the queue can flag repeat-spam senders without
1216	// expanding the PII surface.
1217	model ResponseTriage {
1218	  id               String @id @default(cuid())
1219	  organizationId   String
1220	  publicResponseId String @unique
1221	
1222	  /// SUBSTANTIVE_OBJECTION | CLARIFICATION_REQUEST | GENERAL_ENGAGEMENT | SPAM_NOISE
1223	  label      String
1224	  /// Founder override of `label`. Empty string = honour the classifier label.
1225	  manualLabel String @default("")
1226	
1227	  /// "" | too_short | promotional_link | off_topic | abusive_language | repeat_sender | low_information
1228	  spamReason String @default("")
1229	  /// Founder override of `spamReason` (audit trail; e.g. when un-spamming).
1230	  manualReason String @default("")
1231	
1232	  confidence  Float
1233	  /// One-sentence reduction of the implied objection. Empty unless
1234	  /// label == SUBSTANTIVE_OBJECTION. Feeds the severity rubric.
1235	  impliedObjection String @default("") @db.Text
1236	  rationale        String @default("") @db.Text
1237	  usedLlm          Boolean @default(false)
1238	
1239	  /// SHA-256 of the lowercased submitter email; "" for pseudonymous.
1240	  senderHash          String  @default("")
1241	  elevatedSenderFlag  Boolean @default(false)
1242	
1243	  /// Severity input snapshot from the cascade graph at triage time —
1244	  /// stored so the queue can sort by potential severity without a
1245	  /// fresh cascade traversal on every page render. JSON shape mirrors
1246	  /// `SeverityInputs` in `noosphere/peer_review/severity.py`.
1247	  severityInputsJson String @default("{}")
1248	  /// Cached numeric severity in [0, 1] derived from the snapshot.
1249	  severityValue      Float  @default(0)
1250	
1251	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
1252	  /// Set when the founder archives the row (spam path or
1253	  /// substantive-but-handled). Archived rows drop out of the queue.
1254	  archivedAt   DateTime?
1255	  archiveNote  String    @default("")
1256	
1257	  organization   Organization   @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
1258	  publicResponse PublicResponse @relation(fields: [publicResponseId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
1259	
1260	  @@index([organizationId])
1261	  @@index([organizationId, label])
1262	  @@index([organizationId, archivedAt, severityValue])
1263	  @@index([senderHash])
1264	}
1265	
1266	// `PublicReply` is the founder's reply, attached to a single
1267	// `PublicResponse`. The founder can mark it `private` (emailed back to
1268	// the responder; never published) or `public` (rendered in the article
1269	// appendix). Public replies require BOTH the responder's
1270	// `publishConsent` AND the founder's confirmation here — symmetric
1271	// double opt-in, so neither side can publish unilaterally.
1272	model PublicReply {
1273	  id               String @id @default(cuid())
1274	  organizationId   String
1275	  publicResponseId String @unique
1276	  founderId        String
1277	
1278	  /// private | public
1279	  visibility    String @default("private")
1280	  body          String @db.Text
1281	
1282	  /// True only when the founder explicitly confirmed the publish step
1283	  /// AND the underlying PublicResponse.publishConsent was true at the
1284	  /// time of confirmation. Snapshotted here so a later consent-revoke
1285	  /// does not change the audit record.
1286	  publishConfirmed     Boolean   @default(false)
1287	  publishConfirmedAt   DateTime?
1288	
1289	  /// Whether this reply also opened a review-queue item for the

2080	// counts) computed by folding all prior updates plus this one into
2081	// the source-type prior — denormalised so the UI doesn't have to
2082	// replay the log on every render. The log is the source of truth; if
2083	// the running totals ever disagree with a fold of the rows, the rows
2084	// win.

2086	// `current_credibility` is a view: select the latest row for a
2087	// `sourceId` ordered by `observedAt`.

2090	// `noosphere.literature.standing.canonical_source_id`). `sourceType`
2091	// is one of the values in `noosphere.literature.source_priors.SourceType`;
2092	// it is denormalised on every row so a UI can render the prior label
2093	// without joining to a separate table.

content.text:
Implementation complete. Summary:

**Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`)** — added `AttentionAction` (append-only ledger of snooze/dismiss/unsnooze actions, indexed for both per-founder lookups and per-queue dismissal-rate aggregation) and `Founder.dailyDigestOptIn` flag.

**`src/lib/attention.ts`** — canonical queue vocabulary (8 queues), severity tiers, ranking (`severity desc → age desc → queue id`), `applyFounderActions` (latest action wins; expired snoozes resurface), 14-day snooze cap with `resolveSnoozeRequest` rewriting longer requests as dismissals with reason "deferred indefinitely", `dismissalRateByQueue` tuning signal, and `listAttentionForFounder` orchestrator.

**`src/app/api/founder/attention/route.ts`** — GET returns ranked listing; POST records snooze/dismiss/unsnooze with the same 14-day clamp.

**`src/components/AttentionQueue.tsx` + `AttentionItem.tsx`** — server-renderable queue with per-row client-side snooze/dismiss interactions; tuning hint when a queue accumulates ≥5 dismissals.

**`src/app/(authed)/dashboard/page.tsx`** — `<AttentionPanel>` mounted as the primary surface above the legacy Hearth/Pulse row; existing widgets preserved as secondary.

**`src/lib/dailyDigestEmail.ts`** — opt-in morning digest of open high-severity items, deeplinking to `/dashboard`. Founders with no high-severity items get no email.

**`src/__tests__/AttentionQueue.test.tsx`** — 17 tests covering ranking (severity dominates age, age sorts within tier), `applyFounderActions` (dismiss/snooze/expired snooze/unsnooze), `resolveSnoozeRequest` clamp, synthetic queue mix end-to-end, API audit log (dismiss requires reason; >14d snooze rewritten with "deferred indefinitely" reason; in-cap snoozes recorded as snoozes), and digest email building. All pass; pre-existing failures in unrelated test files are unchanged.
